seoTitle: Change Table Size in Power BI: Quick Layout Fix
metaDescription: Learn how to quickly adjust table size in Power BI to improve readability and make your reports look more polished and professional.
intro: An awkwardly sized table can undercut an otherwise well-designed Power BI report. This guide covers how to access table properties, resize tables cleanly, and avoid common layout mistakes so your data presents clearly.

steps.heading: Access table properties
steps.body: Select your table visual and open its formatting options, where size and layout settings are managed.

steps.heading: Adjust table size
steps.body: Resize the table by dragging its borders or adjusting the relevant formatting settings until it fits well within your report layout.

steps.heading: Avoid common mistakes
steps.body: Watch for issues like cramped columns or excessive white space, which can make a resized table harder to read rather than easier.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for presenting data
steps.body: Keep column widths proportional to their content and leave enough space for readability, especially when the report will be viewed on different screen sizes.

steps.heading: Apply this to your broader reporting workflow
steps.body: Consistent table sizing across your report pages helps create a more professional, cohesive look for anyone reviewing your dashboards.

faq.question: Can I resize a table without affecting the data it displays?
faq.answer: Yes, resizing a table only changes its visual layout and does not alter the underlying data or calculations.

faq.question: Why does my table look cramped after resizing?
faq.answer: This usually happens when column widths aren't adjusted proportionally, so it's worth fine-tuning individual columns after an overall resize.

faq.question: Does table size affect performance in Power BI?
faq.answer: No, resizing a table visual is a cosmetic change and doesn't meaningfully impact your report's performance.

faq.question: Is there a recommended table size for Power BI reports?
faq.answer: There's no single fixed size, since the ideal dimensions depend on your report layout, screen size, and how much data the table needs to display.
